item.title: Recreations of Roman Emperors
item.description:
Augustus - the founder of the Empire
Philip II - less than 12 years old when he ascended to the throne
Galba - governor of upper Germany and Africa. The first in the year of the 4 emperors
Nero - popular among the People but hated by the elite. He was overthrown by the Senate and killed himself
Philip the Arab - ascended from the position of Praetorian Prefect. Rome celebrated it's millennium under his reign. Killed in battle at Verona
Gordian II - only reigned for 21 days. Died in battle outside of Carthage against Maxamimus Thrax
Caligula - son of the famous general Germanicus, Caligula was a cruel tyrant who focused on his sexual perversions. He was assassinated by his own bodyguard
Domitian - a ruthless but efficient autocrat who reformed the coinage and fought wars in Scotland. Assassinated by his court officials
Tiberius - never wanted to be emperor but reigned for 22 years. An able administrator who amassed a huge surplus for the Imperial Treasury
Claudius Gothicus - decisively defeated the Goths at the battle of Naissus. Died during the plague of Cyprian
Trajan - a successful soldier emperor. Known for expanding the empire to it's greatest extent and his philanthropic social welfare programs. The 2nd of the 5 good emperors
Hadrian - a good administrator who solidified the imperial bureaucracy. Known for a wall in northern Britain. The 3rd of the 5 good emperors
Marcus Aurelius - the philosopher emperor. Oversaw monetary reforms and the response to the Antonine plague. The last of the 5 good emperors
Gordian III - Grandson of Gordian I. Killed by the Legio II Parthica while besieged by Maxamimus Thrax
Tacitus - served for less than a year. Died of sickness after he began acting strangely and trying to name all of the months after himself
Vitellius - served less than a year. The 3rd in the year of the 4 emperors.
Lucius Verus - majority of his reign was occupied with war against the Parthians. Died of natural causes
Diocletian - an able soldier-emperor his reign stabilized the empire after the crisis of the 3rd century. He reorganized the provincial governorships and their bureaucracies. Served 20 years and became the 1st emperor to step down and retire
Theodosius I - the last emperor to rule the entire empire before it's administration split between west and east. Established the Nicene Creed. Said to be diligent and austere
Antoninus Pius - 4th of the 5 good emperors. Known for his expansions of aqueducts around the empire and coding laws in the far providences. Died of natural causes
Commodus - the man who came after the 5 good emperors. Typically seen as ending a golden era of mankind known as the Pax Romana. Known as a dictator who indulged his every fantasy
Gordian I - Served for 21 days at the age of 79 before committing suicide. His family origins are unknown
Claudius - infirm his whole life. Conquered Britain. He personally oversaw and adjudicated many public trials.
Valerian - the only emperor to be captured and held as a prisoner of war.
Nerva - the first of the 5 good emperors. He had trouble controlling the military elite. Was considered wise in his lifetime. Choose and heir based on merit and not family ties
Caracalla - murdered his co emperor and brother Geta to assume sole control of the Empire. Built the baths of caracalla
Macaminius Thrax - a so called barracks emperor who never entered the city of Rome. He was the first emperor from neither the senatorial or equestrian class
Maximian - known for his many campaigns on the Rhine frontier against barbarian incursions. Served alongside Diocletian. One of the few emperors to retire.
Titus - Known for building the Coliseum and his response to the eruption of Mount Vesuvius
Otho - Served for 3 months during the year of 4 emperors. Died fighting in Germany.
item.dc:creator: ChortleMcJortle

item.title: As a parent I needed this.
item.description:
I currently have four teenagers, this article punched me in the gut so I figured I’d share it here so I can re-read it whenever I’m at wits end.
Sauce: https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/blog/when-kids-call-the-shots/201801/7-things-teenagers-desperately-need-adults-understand?fbclid=IwAR0lIqocg9yF9ELm4TIBD1KL5ECdJGbf_4tL-rVx76iCWkpcvrVuAfqTc90
item.dc:creator: mikeerk365

item.title: I mean, sure, although it looks like the guy did so to save his own family...
item.description: I could understand naming them if the person had colluded with the Nazis or had given them up for financial gain, but the FBI suggesting it was someone caught in almost the same trap demonstrates what an absolutely shitty position everyone was in. Source: https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-europe-60024228 Top tip for the day: "Never stop punching Nazis" Minor edit: andstabitdead makes a very valid point, the people responsible for this are the Nazis not some Jewish lad trying to save his family. If you are planning on upvoting, do their comment too. It should be the top entry.
item.dc:creator: YouMealyMouthedCrotchPheasant

item.description: Ash and his mom are gonna have to have that talk about the birds and the Beedrills. More: http://alarminglybad.com/linkhub
item.dc:creator: AlarminglyBad

item.title: It's disgusting how grossly misinformed people are about this.
item.description:
Okay okay, this is a bit of a rant post, so TLDR: Reactors arent nukes, and its hard for them to go chernobyl now. We need them if we want to fix the planet. Every time I talk to people online about this people always end up going back to Chernobyl or Fukushima. The truth is it's a lot more complicated than that. Rules about safety are written in blood, thats how everything has worked since the beginning of our civilization. Just like how natural gas is made to smell like rotten eggs because in victorian england it was odorless and caused asphixiation, house fires and explosions. Modern nuclear reactors are built with many contingincies in place to stop nuclear engineers from accidentally causing a meltdown. Now lets talk about chernobyl The soviet government controlling ukraine at the time was hopelessly corrupt, that paired with the severely flawed design of the soviet reactors and human error caused by the engineers working with already outdated technology lead to the worst Nuclear accident in the history of the world. This accident wasnt unforseen however, the nuclear plant had procedures against the actions of the engineers, who ran the plant at extremely low power without following safety precautions and without proper communication. These reactors were also highly unstable at low power due to how they were designed, even so the power plant continued to be used for 14 years after the meltdown until it was shut down in the year 2000, however it remains in decommission to this day. Normally a disaster response would be immediate, but the Soviet government was more concerned with covering up the disaster instead of starting evacuation so many preventable deaths were caused as a result. To sum it up, the chernobyl disaster was caused by a perfect storm of ineptitude from all parties involved. Fukushima is a little less stupid, but was still preventable. The diesel generators that ran the pumps could have easily kept running if they had been built on higher ground. As well when the plant was built they did not pay enough attention to the risk of a tsunami, despite the plant being in a prime spot for such a disaster to occur, had they built proper protections the damage could have been greatly limited. That and the fact that the nuclear agency in charge of the plant botched the response with unnecessary beurocracy. Now lets talk about everyone's favorite talking point.
The forbidden radio cake. Actually this is what nuclear waste looks like Nuclear waste isnt some green sludge that is pumped from reactors into rivers, instead it's the spent Uranium fuel after being reenriched many times to go through the reactor again. After a few times being spent of its usefulness fissile material is packed into dirt and sand, then shipped off for storage. Where you might ask? well we simply stick it right back in the ground where it came from in the first place and seal it up. All that radiation stays in the bedrock and causes almost no pollution if disposed of properly. Remember that uranium is just a metal that we take out of the earth's crust, there's no reason we cant put it back there when we're done with it. Now a lot of people are scared living next to a nuclear power plant will cause you to get radiation poisoning, this actually couldnt be further from the truth. Major accidents are very rare, only 2 class 7 accidents have ever happened in history, so a modern power plant will be totally safe to live near and wont cause your family any problems. What you should be worried about are coal power plants. I know it sounds weird but a coal power plant actually emits more radiation into its surrounding area than a nuclear power plant ever will in its normal operating life. Nuclear power plants are also safer than all of our other forms of power generation, while fossil fuel cause tens of thousands of deaths a year, nuclear energy causes less than 1 death every year due to how safe it's become. If we're going to fix our environment, we have to use the resources we have at our disposal now, we have a clean and safe was to produce all of our energy but we're not using it, Nuclear power plants have a tiny carbon footprint and are reliable enough to completely replace fossil fuels, we just need to actually use them instead of shutting them all down.
item.dc:creator: FistingWithJalapenoDoritoFingers

item.title: keto weight loss & mock potato soup
item.description:
cook bacon bits
consult sous chef on doneness
set aside for later
i left all the bacon grease because yolo, add two celery stalks and one yellow onion
add salt and pepper and i added butter ??, cook for ten min on medium
add whole head of cauli flower, and minced garlic (i did five cloves ?) cover in broth of your choice
After the cauliflower is soft as f immersion blend (i took out about a 1/4 cup of the broth before blended to retain thiccness)
Add cheese and heavy cream and then blend again
Garnish with the bacon, some more cheese, green onions, and some crème fraiche

item.title: Fuck dying … and fuck the dying
item.description: … I’m the dying Dying sucks, it gives prostitutes a run for their money for how much it suck … and, unlike prostitutes, it’s not the fun kind of sucking. So another person, a total 10/10 hottie no less, finding me attractive when I feel literally decrepit is pretty great honestly. I barely managed to hold back my tears until she was out of earshot. Thanks for letting me vent to you wonderful strangers. Edit: thank you to the ladies, gents, and assorted others of Imgur who have offered to send me pic of various body parts … it’s greatly appreciated, and y’all look hot AF
item.dc:creator: whangobango

item.title: Neighbor is missing
item.description: My neighbors granddaughter, who he raises, went missing this evening. My daughter used to play with her when they were younger. Just sending this out in case it has even a slim chance of finding the right eyeballs to try and find her.

item.title: That Smell [OC]
item.description: I haven't done a remaster in a while! I set out just updating the art, but the bodies of the zebra and chameleon were a pain to get right. I realized if I made the panels short and wide, I could avoid drawing their bodies, suggest more of a crowd at the party and make the characters larger! Larger characters = more obvious expressis the before (2019) and after: https://twitter.com/LitterboxComics/status/1483132486263754755?s=20
item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics
